title: Crown Him with Many Crowns
author: Matthew Bridges / Godfrey Thring

scriptureRefs.note: On his head were many crowns
Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power

story: Matthew Bridges, who converted from Anglicanism to Roman Catholicism, originally wrote six stanzas in 1851. Godfrey Thring, an Anglican clergyman, felt the hymn needed additional verses from a Protestant perspective and wrote six more stanzas in 1874. Most hymnals today use a combination of both authors' verses. The majestic tune DIADEMATA by George Job Elvey was composed in 1868 specifically for this text.

lines: Hark! how the heavenly anthem drowns
Of Him who died for thee,
And hail Him as thy matchless King
Crown Him the Lord of love;
But downward bends his burning eye
Crown Him the Lord of peace,
From pole to pole, that wars may cease,
His reign shall know no end,
Crown Him the Lord of life,
And rose victorious in the strife
For those He came to save;
Who died, and rose on high,
Who died, eternal life to bring,
And lives that death may die.
